title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Between 2013 and 2024, the life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians doubled from 3.3 to 6.6 years.
Black Bostonians face a 76.2-year life expectancy, compared to 82.2 years for other Boston residents.
Boston Public Health Commission announced $2 million investment for Black men's health and Dana-Farber Cancer Institute collaboration.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Night' promotion set for March 16.
Kornet expressed concerns over promoting Magic City, a strip club, without acknowledging its nature in the event announcement.
Kornet urged the NBA and fans to consider the promotion's implications on women's respect and safety in the league.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carlolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, from Bristol, R.I., were killed in a crash in Swansea on Feb. 27.
The crash occurred at the intersection of Route 136 and Route 6, involving Demitri Sousa's vehicle and the Carcasis' Subaru.
Demitri Sousa, 28, the alleged driver who hit the couple, is also a suspect in a prior fatal shooting in Rhode Island.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for discussing Kristaps Porzingis' health condition, acknowledging he wasn't qualified to speak on the diagnosis.
Kristaps Porzingis was limited to 18 games this season due to lingering illness, following a POTS diagnosis over the offseason.
Porzingis scored 12 points in his only game with the Golden State Warriors after being traded from the Atlanta Hawks.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins could trade draft picks, players like Mason Lohrei and Andrew Peeke before March 6 NHL deadline.
Boston holds multiple first-round picks, including 2026 Toronto's pick, offering potential for trades.
Players such as Viktor Arvidsson, Matt Poitras, and Casey Mittelstadt are potential trade assets.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby could be traded this week, with the Patriots among interested teams, says NFL insiders Adam Schefter and Albert Breer.
Several teams, including Dallas, Chicago, Baltimore, Buffalo, Philadelphia, and the Rams, are monitoring Crosby's situation, according to reports.
The Raiders, coming off a 3-14 season, hold the No. 1 draft pick; Crosby's potential trade has generated significant interest.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Quincy man Sanusi Sadiq sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in 2023 St. Patrick's Day assault.
Victim Barry Whelan, 46, died of blunt force trauma after being struck and falling in Downtown Boston.
First trial ended in mistrial; Sadiq apologized for incident citing poor judgment during his sentencing.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons suggests Jayson Tatum may return Friday against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
Tatum has not been cleared for games, remaining out with Achilles tendon repair against Milwaukee.
ESPN reports Tatum has resumed 5-on-5 practice, but Celtics announce no timeline for his return.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court will hear arguments on the legality of handgun possession for those under 21.
The case follows a US Supreme Court decision that could affect gun laws, particularly for 18- to 20-year-olds.
Gun-rights advocates claim age restrictions violate the Second Amendment; prosecutors argue they ensure public safety.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney is considering trade deadline options to strengthen the team without sacrificing future prospects.
Sweeney emphasized avoiding short-term rental players, focusing instead on potential long-term, prime-contract talent additions.
The team has significant draft capital, holding four first-round picks over the next two years, to strategize future trades.
